text_content: The purpose of this UAP is to: Identify deliverables, scope, organization, roles and responsibilities, content strategy and information architecture, managerial process, tasks, schedule, milestones, and technical process required to provide effective documentation for users of the product. UA included in this release is listed at section 2. For details on the UA to be created or updated for this release, see section 5, Detailed Project Scope.

source: D58308 HAL 4.0 Device Description Rev 1.0.docx.pdf
title: 1.0 Executive Summary > 1.2 Device Classification > 1.3.6 Operator Profiles (Continued)
text_content: The hospital technician, typically a biomedical engineer, is a local, in-clinic resource that is responsible for maintaining the treatment delivery system and associated software and hardware. This resource is trained to a defined level of competency and is provided with limited-service access to the system. The role of hospital technician is to maintain operation of the treatment delivery system and is the first line of support before contacting the Varian Customer Support Representative and /or will work with the Varian Customer Support Representative to help resolve hardware issues. The system is an assortment of components which can be a combination of hardware and software. When preparing hardware for clinical use, the hospital technician may calibrate the hardware and ensure the accuracy of the hardware parameters. Americas: Physicist, Dosimetrist, Radiation Therapist APAC: Physicist, Radiation Physicist, Medical Physicist EMEA: Physicist

title: 2. Validation Overview > 2.2.4 Installation/Upgrade Testing
text_content: As part of Validation TRR (merge Quote to Cash (Q2C) activities), Product Life Cycle Services (PLCS) will work with Varian Installation Team to perform validation testing of the HAL v4.0 associated Service Technical Instructions (STI’s).  The STI’s are the Installation and Service documentation required to install, upgrade, and maintain the new SW and HW for the device under test. PLCS will work closely with the Installation Team and Global Customer Support to identify candidates to participate in the validation testing of the installation and upgrade documentation. PLCS will use engineering systems and, if available, non-clinical systems to develop the Service Technical Instructions (STI’s) and to perform the validation testing.  Perform installation and upgrades as the User. title: 15. User defined CBCT modes can be exported, imported, or deleted. > 5.1 Actor
text_content: The primary actor for maintaining the operation of the equipment is the Hospital Technician: The Hospital Technician is trained and/or certified individual responsible for maintaining the operation of the hardware. Typically, the Hospital Technician is the first line of support before contacting the Customer Support Representative and /or will work with the Customer Support Representative to help resolve hardware issues.  The Hospital Technician would be expected to attend a training program provided by a vendor or will obtain on the job training during installation and servicing. The Hospital Technician is not responsible for maintaining clinical correctness of the hardware, only the operation of the hardware. Americas: Biomedical Engineer, Physicist, 3rd Party Contractor APAC: Biomedical Engineer, Physicist, 3rd Party Contractor EMEA: Biomedical Engineer, Physicist, 3rd Party Contractor

source: D78333 Ethos 4.0 Description of interoperability Study Rev01.docx.pdf
title: 1. Basic Information > 2.2 Interoperation Maintenance
text_content: Support & Maintenance Process Customers and any employee that experience erroneous behavior are advised to report these errors to the business unit that has sold the TPS system. These reports are analyzed by the respective complaint handling department. Every error is inserted into the corrective and preventive action (CAPA) and complaint handling procedures and, where necessary, is reproduced in the laboratory for analysis. The complaint handling and the CAPA procedures ensure that the complaints are assessed for the need of Medical Device Reporting (MDR). Also ensured are a root cause analysis of the erroneous behavior and the provision of appropriate change requests for fixing the error. Depending on the significance of the problem, required changes are made by returning to the appropriate development state and implementing appropriate fixes. The changes are then retested and re-evaluated and are either distributed as updates through standard service

source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 4.0 Requirements for Risk Management Activities and their Review (Continued)
text_content: FMEA sessions require a cross-functional and technically biased team approach. FMEA analysis relies on domain knowledge of the team to identify components to consider and specific manner or way by which a failure occurs.  Analysis is conducted thru brainstorming sessions by the team.   Manner or way by which a failure occurs is highly domain (mechanical, electrical, or software) specific. Process FMEA investigates the risk in manufacturing and the supply chain that may cause material or product not meeting requirements, manufacturing operation impact or manufacturing operator impact. Contributing documents such as specifications, requirements, reports, investigations, and studies shall be reviewed by persons with recorded competence in the documents’ subjects. Where relevant, these persons shall have knowledge and experience with the device(s) in this RMP’s scope as well as familiarity with the device’s use(s) and technologies.

General content – Maintaining UA (updates for corrections or changes from the prior release) and updating overall content not specific to features. CRNs – Formatting, finalizing content for publication, and publishing. Quality assurance and production – Reviews, including formal and technical (DITA XML QA) reviews, and revisions per review findings. Management and localization – Administration, meetings, coordination with GPL Localization on translation work, distribution.
